name: Used cars
summary: Used vehicle purchases — high-stakes, lemon-law-protected category.

evidence.claim: FTC Used Car Rule (16 CFR Part 455) requires used-car dealers to display a Buyers Guide on every used vehicle disclosing whether it is sold 'As-Is' or 'With Warranty', the warranty terms, and the major mechanical and electrical systems on the car.
State lemon laws cover used cars in some jurisdictions but not all. The federal Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act provides additional remedies if the dealer made any written warranty.
NHTSA recall lookup at nhtsa.gov/recalls allows VIN-based recall search; ~25% of used cars on the market have at least one open recall as of 2024.

criteria.name: title_status
criteria.notes: Anything other than 'clean' is a major red flag.

confabulationPatterns.reality: CPO standards vary widely by manufacturer. Toyota, Lexus, Honda CPO programs are rigorous (150-200 point inspections, 7-year/100k extended warranty). Generic dealer 'certified' (not manufacturer-backed) is just a cosmetic detail; verify the program.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the certification manufacturer-backed (e.g. Toyota CPO) or dealer-only?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Means 'no reported accidents' on Carfax/AutoCheck. Many minor accidents go unreported. Pre-purchase inspection by independent mechanic is required regardless.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the user obtained a pre-purchase inspection from a mechanic NOT affiliated with the seller?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Sometimes true but doesn't equal good condition; rental cars often have one owner (Hertz/Enterprise) but heavy use. Always check service records.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the one owner a private party or fleet/rental company?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Sub-50k miles on a 10-year-old car suggests rare driving — could be sat-and-deteriorated (rubber seals, fluids, battery). Not always a positive.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: How was the car driven (commute vs occasional)? Sit-time can be worse than miles.

counterfeitSignals.signal: Title doesn't match seller name; dealer transferring 'title later'
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — title-jumping is illegal in most states; walk away

counterfeitSignals.signal: Price >25% below comparable market value with vague history
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — likely flood, salvage, or stolen-recovered

compatibilityQuestions: What's your annual mileage? Some cars have known high-mileage failure points (CVT transmissions, chain timing).
EV vs ICE vs hybrid — depends on charging access at home + commute.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Sales tax + title + registration (~10-12% of price)
First-year repairs on used car (budget 5-10% of purchase price)

repairability.notes: Most cars 8-15 years old have strong parts markets. EV battery replacement is the major exception ($3k-$15k).
